metadata.subgroup_label: Knowledge as inner content and covenantal knowing
metadata.subgroup_description: Terms that name knowledge as both inner content held in the knowing self and the constitutive relational orientation of knowing God. Includes the covenantal knowing of God and its structural opposite (spiritual ignorance/agnoeō).

verse_context_groups.label: God's omniscient knowing (his attribute)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names God's comprehensive, self-sufficient knowing of all things — hearts, plans, character, deeds. No one gave him this knowing; his understanding is unsearchable. Distinct from his elective knowing of persons (VCG02) and his redemptive self-disclosure through acts (VCG03). Inner-being phenomenon: divine omniscient attribute.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Psa 139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high ; I cannot attain it .
anchor_verse.meaning: da.at names God's comprehensive knowledge of the psalmist — too vast to be comprehended by the one who is known. The knowing is God's; its object is the psalmist.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: God's knowing of the psalmist stated in its most personal and overwhelming form: 'Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high; I cannot attain it.' The verse names the attribute (God's knowing of the inner person), its quality (too wonderful to comprehend), and its effect (the human person cannot reach or contain it). The phenomenon — omniscient divine knowing — is stated at its most direct.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 2:3 Talk no more so very proudly , let not arrogance come from your mouth ; for the Lord is a God of knowledge , and by him actions are weighed .
verses.meaning: de.ah names knowledge as a divine attribute — God's perfect, comprehensive knowing that weighs all human action.

verses.verse_text: Psa 73:11 And they say , “ How can God know ? Is there knowledge in the Most High ?”
verses.meaning: de.ah names the divine knowing that the wicked deny — they claim God does not know, thereby refusing moral scrutiny.

verses.verse_text: Job 10:7 although you know that I am not guilty , and there is none to deliver out of your hand ?
verses.meaning: da.at names God's knowledge of Job's innocence — the inner moral condition that God knows but apparently acts against.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:22 Will any teach God knowledge , seeing that he judges those who are on high ?
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that belongs to God — no one can instruct God; his knowing is self-sufficient and above all judgment.

verses.verse_text: Job 22:13 But you say , ‘ What does God know ? Can he judge through the deep darkness ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being denied to God — the challenge to divine omniscience by those who think he cannot see.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:10 But he knows the way that I take ; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowledge of Job's path — the comfort is that God knows even when Job cannot find him.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:15 Do you know how God lays his command upon them and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's governing command — knowledge of how God directs natural phenomena is beyond human reach.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner capacity that the divine challenge exposes as absent — the term identifies knowing the balancings of the clouds as the knowledge required to answer God.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,
verses.meaning: de.a names perfect knowledge as a divine attribute — God's inner knowing of his own works is complete.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:4 For truly my words are not false ; one who is perfect in knowledge is with you .
verses.meaning: de.ah names perfect knowledge as a divine attribute — Elihu either applies it to himself (boldly) or to God. Either way, perfect de.ah is a standard of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:13 Who has measured the Spirit of the Lord , or what man shows him his counsel ?
verses.meaning: Names the scope of divine self-sufficiency — the term identifies making known his counsel as the divine prerogative that no one has exercised on God's behalf.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: Names the modes of inner instruction — the term identifies understanding and knowledge as what God was taught by no one, establishing his unrivalled inner competence.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge no one taught God — his knowing is original and uncreated.

verses.verse_text: Isa 37:28 “‘I know your sitting down and your going out and coming in , and your raging against me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of the Assyrian king — every movement and attitude is known.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high ; I cannot attain it .
verses.meaning: da.at names God's comprehensive knowledge of the psalmist — too vast to be comprehended by the one who is known. The knowing is God's; its object is the psalmist.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:4 Because I know that you are obstinate , and your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead brass ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Israel's character — inner knowing of their stubbornness as the reason for the way he addressed them.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:8 You have never heard , you have never known , from of old your ear has not been opened . For I knew that you would surely deal treacherously , and that from before birth you were called a rebel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's foreknowing of Israel's rebellion — comprehensive inner knowledge of their character before they acted.

verses.verse_text: Amo 5:12 For I know how many are your transgressions and how great are your sins — you who afflict the righteous , who take a bribe , and turn aside the needy in the gate .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive moral knowing of Israel's sin — specific and complete.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:11 And why ? Because I do not love you ? God knows I do!
verses.meaning: oida names God's comprehensive inner knowing of Paul's love — appealed to as the ultimate witness.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:31 The God and Father of the Lord Jesus , he who is blessed forever , knows that I am not lying .
verses.meaning: oida names God's knowing as the ground of Paul's oath — divine inner knowing is the ultimate guarantor.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:11 Therefore , knowing the fear of the Lord , we persuade others . But what we are is known to God , and I hope it is known also to your conscience .
verses.meaning: oida names self-knowledge of inner motivations — what Paul is, is known to God, and hopefully to conscience.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:27 And he who searches hearts knows what is the mind of the Spirit , because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God .
verses.meaning: oida names God's comprehensive inner knowing of persons and the Spirit's mind — searching hearts is the divine activity of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:27 For I know how rebellious and stubborn you are. Behold , even today while I am yet alive with you, you have been rebellious against the Lord . How much more after my death !
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's knowing of Israel's character — comprehensive knowledge of their inner disposition.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:29 For I know that after my death you will surely act corruptly and turn aside from the way that I have commanded you. And in the days to come evil will befall you, because you will do what is evil in the sight of the Lord , provoking him to anger through the work of your hands .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Moses's prophetic knowledge of what will happen — inner conviction about future covenant unfaithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:10 and performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land , for you knew that they acted arrogantly against our fathers. And you made a name for yourself, as it is to this day .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Pharaoh's arrogance — inner covenantal knowing of the oppressor's character and act.

verses.verse_text: Gen 20:6 Then God said to him in the dream , “ Yes , I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart , and it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive inner knowledge of Abimelech's motives — the integrity of the heart is known to God.

verses.verse_text: Gen 18:21 I will go down to see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me. And if not , I will know .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's investigative knowing — not ignorance but the divine process of attending to and fully comprehending the situation.

verses.verse_text: Hos 5:3 I know Ephraim , and Israel is not hidden from me; for now, O Ephraim , you have played the whore ; Israel is defiled .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowing of the nation's condition — their sin is fully visible to him.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:10 He who disciplines the nations , does he not rebuke ? He who teaches man knowledge —
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a divine gift God teaches to humans — an inner endowment transmitted by God.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;
verses.meaning: man.da names knowledge as God's gift to those who have understanding — the inner capacity flows from God to receptive persons.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:20 by his knowledge the deeps broke open , and the clouds drop down the dew .
verses.meaning: Names the active understanding by which God ordered creation — the term identifies divine knowledge as the inner faculty through which the cosmic deeps were opened.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:26 Behold , God is great , and we know him not ; the number of his years is unsearchable .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that human limitation cannot reach — God's greatness exceeds human knowing.

verses.verse_text: Zec 14:7 And there shall be a unique day , which is known to the Lord , neither day nor night , but at evening time there shall be light .
verses.meaning: Names the exclusive divine awareness — the term identifies the unique coming day as one that is known only to the Lord, beyond human foreknowledge.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:8 Fear not , nor be afraid ; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses ! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock ; I know not any.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's self-knowledge — his inner knowing finds no other God. The divine self-knowledge is the ground of monotheism.

verses.verse_text: Job 31:6 (Let me be weighed in a just balance , and let God know my integrity !)
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's appeal to God's knowing of his inner moral state — calling for divine knowledge of his integrity as grounds for vindication.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:21 And when many evils and troubles have come upon them, this song shall confront them as a witness ( for it will live unforgotten in the mouths of their offspring ). For I know what they are inclined to do even today , before I have brought them into the land that I swore to give.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive foreknowing of Israel's future rebellion — the inner tendency of the people known by God before the event.

verses.verse_text: Exo 2:25 God saw the people of Israel —and God knew .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's covenantal attending — the knowing is relational recognition of his covenant people in their suffering.

verses.verse_text: Exo 3:19 But I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless compelled by a mighty hand .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's foreknowing of Pharaoh's resistance — inner knowledge of the king's character and what will be required.

verses.verse_text: Jos 22:22 “The Mighty One , God , the Lord ! The Mighty One , God , the Lord ! He knows ; and let Israel itself know ! If it was in rebellion or in breach of faith against the Lord , do not spare us today
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowing — the appeal to God as the one who knows the inner truth of the situation.

verse_context_groups.label: God's elective / covenantal knowing of persons
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names God's knowing as an act of electing, choosing, and entering into intimate covenantal relationship — face to face knowing, knowing in the wilderness, 'You only have I known of all the families of the earth.' This is not God's omniscient knowing of everyone but his specific relational knowing that constitutes covenant identity. Inner-being phenomenon: divine elective knowing.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Amo 3:2 “You only have I known of all the families of the earth ; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities .
anchor_verse.meaning: ya.da names God's elective covenantal knowing of Israel — uniquely known, uniquely accountable. One of the defining statements of election as knowing.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'You only have I known of all the families of the earth; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.' The most concentrated statement of election-as-knowing in Scripture. The verb's force is completely clear: knowing = choosing = entering covenant relationship. Its consequence (accountability) shows the weight of what this knowing establishes.

verses.verse_text: Gen 18:19 For I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing righteousness and justice , so that the Lord may bring to Abraham what he has promised him .”
verses.meaning: ya.da here is rendered "chosen" — God's knowing of Abraham is an election knowing, the covenantal intimacy that results in appointment and purpose.

verses.verse_text: Gen 22:12 He said , “Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God , seeing you have not withheld your son , your only son, from me .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's knowing confirmed through action — the test has produced knowledge of Abraham's character. Covenantal knowing verified through obedience.

verses.verse_text: Exo 2:4 And his sister stood at a distance to know what would be done to him .
verses.meaning: Names the inner purpose of watching — the term identifies the sister's reason for positioning herself: to know what would happen to the child.

verses.verse_text: Amo 3:2 “You only have I known of all the families of the earth ; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's elective covenantal knowing of Israel — uniquely known, uniquely accountable. One of the defining statements of election as knowing.

verses.verse_text: Gen 21:26 Abimelech said , “I do not know who has done this thing ; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Abimelech's genuine ignorance of the well's origin, which he had neither done nor been told about.

verses.verse_text: Nah 1:7 The Lord is good , a stronghold in the day of trouble ; he knows those who take refuge in him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's covenantal knowing of his own — those who come to him are personally known to him.

verses.verse_text: Job 24:1 “ Why are not times of judgment kept by the Almighty , and why do those who know him never see his days ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal relationship of those who know God — those in relationship with him who cannot understand his timing.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:5 It was I who knew you in the wilderness , in the land of drought ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's covenantal election knowing of Israel in their most vulnerable place — intimate relational knowing in the desert.

verses.verse_text: Deu 34:10 And there has not arisen a prophet since in Israel like Moses , whom the Lord knew face to face ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the intimate covenantal knowing between God and Moses — face to face knowing as the highest form of prophetic relationship.

verse_context_groups.label: God making himself known through acts
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names God's redemptive self-disclosure through historical events — 'that you/they may know that I am the Lord.' The pattern runs through Exodus, the wilderness, the Jordan crossing, the prophets, and the apocalyptic oracles. The goal is always inner recognition produced by the event. Inner-being phenomenon: God's self-revealing disclosure through history.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Jos 4:24 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of the Lord is mighty , that you may fear the Lord your God forever .”
anchor_verse.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition that the miracle aims to produce — all peoples knowing God's power.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: The explicit statement of God's purpose in the Jordan crossing: 'so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of the Lord is mighty, that you may fear the Lord your God forever.' Names the event, the intended inner knowing it will produce, the scope (all peoples), and the relational goal (fear = covenant orientation). The pattern of the entire VCG stated in a single verse.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:6 You have not eaten bread , and you have not drunk wine or strong drink , that you may know that I am the Lord your God .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the goal of the wilderness provision — the events produce inner covenantal recognition of God's identity.

verses.verse_text: Joe 2:14 Who knows whether he will not turn and relent , and leave a blessing behind him, a grain offering and a drink offering for the Lord your God ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the divine freedom before human action — no one can claim to know what God will do; he is not compelled.

verses.verse_text: Jos 3:7 The Lord said to Joshua , “ Today I will begin to exalt you in the sight of all Israel , that they may know that, as I was with Moses , so I will be with you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's presence with Joshua that the exalting sign will produce — covenantal self-disclosure through event.

verses.verse_text: Jos 3:10 And Joshua said , “ Here is how you shall know that the living God is among you and that he will without fail drive out from before you the Canaanites , the Hittites , the Hivites , the Perizzites , the Girgashites , the Amorites , and the Jebusites .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's presence that the crossing will demonstrate — covenantal knowing through miraculous event.

verses.verse_text: Jos 4:22 then you shall let your children know , ‘ Israel passed over this Jordan on dry ground .’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the transmission of covenantal memory — making known what God did so the next generation knows him through the telling.

verses.verse_text: Jos 4:24 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of the Lord is mighty , that you may fear the Lord your God forever .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition that the miracle aims to produce — all peoples knowing God's power.

verses.verse_text: Jos 22:31 And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest said to the people of Reuben and the people of Gad and the people of Manasseh , “ Today we know that the Lord is in our midst , because you have not committed this breach of faith against the Lord . Now you have delivered the people of Israel from the hand of the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's covenant presence produced by the clarification — relational recognition restored.

verses.verse_text: Jos 23:13 know for certain that the Lord your God will no longer drive out these nations before you, but they shall be a snare and a trap for you, a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes , until you perish from off this good ground that the Lord your God has given you.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of consequence — what Israel must hold inwardly as certain.

verses.verse_text: Jos 23:14 “And now I am about to go the way of all the earth , and you know in your hearts and souls , all of you, that not one word has failed of all the good things that the Lord your God promised concerning you. All have come to pass for you; not one of them has failed .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal inner knowing — held in the heart and soul, confirmed by experience.

verses.verse_text: Jos 24:31 Israel served the Lord all the days of Joshua , and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua and had known all the work that the Lord did for Israel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's mighty acts — those who had lived through them and knew God through them.

verses.verse_text: Jon 3:9 Who knows ? God may turn and relent and turn from his fierce anger , so that we may not perish .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the open covenantal possibility — no one can know for certain what God will do; the appeal to him is worth making.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 6:3 They said , “If you send away the ark of the God of Israel , do not send it empty , but by all means return him a guilt offering . Then you will be healed , and it will be known to you why his hand does not turn away from you .”
verses.meaning: Names the desired inner recognition — the term identifies what will become known to the Philistines — the reason God's hand has not turned away — if they return the ark with a guilt offering.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 6:9 and watch . If it goes up on the way to its own land , to Beth-shemesh , then it is he who has done us this great harm , but if not , then we shall know that it is not his hand that struck us ; it happened to us by coincidence .”
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of discernment — the term identifies the test by which the Philistines will know whether their affliction is divine or coincidental.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 12:17 Is it not wheat harvest today ? I will call upon the Lord , that he may send thunder and rain . And you shall know and see that your wickedness is great , which you have done in the sight of the Lord , in asking for yourselves a king .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of sin that the sign will produce — the people will know their own wrongdoing through God's response.

verses.verse_text: Neh 6:16 And when all our enemies heard of it, all the nations around us were afraid and fell greatly in their own esteem , for they perceived that this work had been accomplished with the help of our God .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine action — the enemies perceive God's involvement in the work.

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:14 and you made known to them your holy Sabbath and commanded them commandments and statutes and a law by Moses your servant .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's revelatory self-disclosure — making the Sabbath known as a gift of divine self-revelation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 12:4 And you will say in that day : “Give thanks to the Lord , call upon his name , make known his deeds among the peoples , proclaim that his name is exalted .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the transmission of covenantal knowledge — proclaiming among the nations what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Isa 12:5 “Sing praises to the Lord , for he has done gloriously ; let this be made known in all the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal disclosure of God's glorious acts — covenantal self-disclosure through proclamation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:21 And the Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians , and the Egyptians will know the Lord in that day and worship with sacrifice and offering , and they will make vows to the Lord and perform them.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal self-disclosure of God to a foreign nation and their resulting knowing — a remarkable eschatological extension of covenant knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 37:20 So now , O Lord our God , save us from his hand , that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition that Hezekiah's prayer aims for — all nations knowing God's uniqueness through his deliverance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 38:19 The living , the living , he thanks you, as I do this day ; the father makes known to the children your faithfulness .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the intergenerational transmission of covenantal knowing — parents passing on knowledge of God's faithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:19 Behold , I am doing a new thing ; now it springs forth , do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal perception of what God is doing — the new thing requires inner perception to be received.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:3 I will give you the treasures of darkness and the hoards in secret places , that you may know that it is I , the Lord , the God of Israel , who call you by your name .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's identity that the treasures will produce in Cyrus — an extraordinary extension of covenantal self-disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:6 that people may know , from the rising of the sun and from the west , that there is none besides me; I am the Lord , and there is no other .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition of God's uniqueness as the goal of history — all peoples knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:7 They are created now , not long ago ; before today you have never heard of them, lest you should say , ‘ Behold , I knew them .’
verses.meaning: Names the anticipated false claim of prior inner knowledge — the term identifies the danger that Israel might claim to have already known the new things, which God preempts.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:19 who say : “Let him be quick , let him speed his work that we may see it; let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw near , and let it come , that we may know it!”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's counsel being sought ironically — the mockers demand what they refuse to receive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:5 And now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard . I will remove its hedge , and it shall be devoured ; I will break down its wall , and it shall be trampled down .
verses.meaning: Names the declared inner intent — the term identifies God's disclosure of what he will do as an act of making known his purpose to those addressed.

verses.verse_text: Joe 2:27 You shall know that I am in the midst of Israel , and that I am the Lord your God and there is none else . And my people shall never again be put to shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's presence produced by restoration.

verses.verse_text: Joe 3:17 “So you shall know that I am the Lord your God , who dwells in Zion , my holy mountain . And Jerusalem shall be holy , and strangers shall never again pass through it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's dwelling — knowing him in his holy mountain.

verses.verse_text: Zec 2:9 “ Behold , I will shake my hand over them, and they shall become plunder for those who served them. Then you will know that the Lord of hosts has sent me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine mission that the act will produce.

verses.verse_text: Zec 2:11 And many nations shall join themselves to the Lord in that day , and shall be my people . And I will dwell in your midst , and you shall know that the Lord of hosts has sent me to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same covenantal recognition — repeated in the Zechariah oracles.

verses.verse_text: Zec 4:9 “The hands of Zerubbabel have laid the foundation of this house ; his hands shall also complete it. Then you will know that the Lord of hosts has sent me to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition that the completion of the temple will produce.

verses.verse_text: Zec 6:15 “And those who are far off shall come and help to build the temple of the Lord . And you shall know that the Lord of hosts has sent me to you. And this shall come to pass, if you will diligently obey the voice of the Lord your God .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition produced by the distant builders' coming to help — the pattern is repeated throughout Zechariah.

verses.verse_text: Mal 2:4 So shall you know that I have sent this command to you, that my covenant with Levi may stand, says the Lord of hosts .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of divine authority that the fulfilment will produce.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:13 Then the Lord said to Abram , “ Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's disclosure to Abram of what is coming — covenantal foreknowledge given as inner certainty.

verses.verse_text: Isa 49:26 I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh , and they shall be drunk with their own blood as with wine . Then all flesh shall know that I am the Lord your Savior , and your Redeemer , the Mighty One of Jacob .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal knowing of God's identity produced by judgment on oppressors.

verses.verse_text: Isa 52:6 Therefore my people shall know my name . Therefore in that day they shall know that it is I who speak ; here I am.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's name as a future promise — Israel will know who he is by name.

verses.verse_text: Judg 13:21 The angel of the Lord appeared no more to Manoah and to his wife . Then Manoah knew that he was the angel of the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition produced by the ascending flame — seeing the divine act produces knowing of the divine identity.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:4 They were for the testing of Israel , to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of the Lord , which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that the testing will produce — covenantal obedience made visible and knowable.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:10 Then the men were exceedingly afraid and said to him, “ What is this that you have done !” For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord , because he had told them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the pagan sailors' recognition of Jonah's situation — they come to covenantal knowledge of the Lord through the events.

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:2 Day to day pours out speech , and night to night reveals knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that creation pours out — the heavens disclose inner knowing of God's glory. Note: assigned to 1838-001 (culpable ignorance), which does not fit. This is positive disclosure of knowledge, not its absence.

verses.verse_text: Hos 5:9 Ephraim shall become a desolation in the day of punishment ; among the tribes of Israel I make known what is sure .
verses.meaning: Names the act of disclosure — the term identifies God making known what is sure among the tribes as a form of judicial announcement within Israel.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:29 be afraid of the sword , for wrath brings the punishment of the sword , that you may know there is a judgment .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of divine judgment that the warning aims to produce — inner recognition of moral accountability.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:7 And they said to one another , “ Come , let us cast lots , that we may know on whose account this evil has come upon us.” So they cast lots , and the lot fell on Jonah .
verses.meaning: Names the goal of the lot — the term identifies knowing on whose account the evil has come as the information the lot is meant to disclose.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:14 Let the young woman to whom I shall say , ‘ Please let down your jar that I may drink ,’ and who shall say , ‘ Drink , and I will water your camels ’—let her be the one whom you have appointed for your servant Isaac . By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's covenantal faithfulness that the sign will confirm — recognition of steadfast love through a particular event.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:21 The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether the Lord had prospered his journey or not .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the discerning attention directed toward discovering God's will — attentive observation as a form of knowing.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 10:8 Then go down before me to Gilgal . And behold , I am coming down to you to offer burnt offerings and to sacrifice peace offerings . Seven days you shall wait , until I come to you and show you what you shall do .”
verses.meaning: Names the communicated inner direction — the term identifies what God will show Samuel to do, framed as knowledge to be imparted.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:3 And invite Jesse to the sacrifice , and I will show you what you shall do . And you shall anoint for me him whom I declare to you .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names divine disclosure of the specific act required — God will make known through the occasion what Samuel must do.

verses.verse_text: Gen 20:7 Now then, return the man’s wife , for he is a prophet , so that he will pray for you , and you shall live . But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die , you and all who are yours.”
verses.meaning: Names the certain inner knowledge that comes as a warning — the term identifies the knowledge of inevitable death that Abimelech is given if he fails to restore Sarah.

verses.verse_text: Hab 3:2 O Lord , I have heard the report of you, and your work, O Lord , do I fear . In the midst of the years revive it ; in the midst of the years make it known ; in wrath remember mercy .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the appeal for divine self-disclosure — Habakkuk asks God to make his work known again in the current time.

verses.verse_text: Hos 9:7 The days of punishment have come ; the days of recompense have come ; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool ; the man of the spirit is mad , because of your great iniquity and great hatred .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of judgment that Israel will receive — they will know the days of punishment by experiencing them.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:13 Hear , you who are far off , what I have done ; and you who are near , acknowledge my might .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's power — those near are called to inner acknowledgment of his acts.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:22 Let them bring them, and tell us what is to happen . Tell us the former things , what they are, that we may consider them, that we may know their outcome ; or declare to us the things to come .
verses.meaning: Names the inner goal of prophetic disclosure — the term identifies knowing the outcome of former things as the purpose for which Israel challenges the idols to speak.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:23 Tell us what is to come hereafter , that we may know that you are gods ; do good , or do harm , that we may be dismayed and terrified .
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of divine identity — the term identifies knowing the future as the test by which the idols' deity would be established.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:26 Who declared it from the beginning , that we might know , and beforehand , that we might say , “He is right ”? There was none who declared it, none who proclaimed , none who heard your words .
verses.meaning: Names the inner recognition that accurate prophecy would produce — the term identifies knowing that the speaker is right as the effect that prior declaration would have achieved.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:6 “You have heard ; now see all this; and will you not declare it? From this time forth I announce to you new things , hidden things that you have not known .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the new divine disclosures — things God is now revealing that were previously unknown.

verses.verse_text: Isa 49:23 Kings shall be your foster fathers , and their queens your nursing mothers . With their faces to the ground they shall bow down to you, and lick the dust of your feet . Then you will know that I am the Lord ; those who wait for me shall not be put to shame .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's identity produced by the restoration — those who waited will know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 9:9 and all the people will know , Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria , who say in pride and in arrogance of heart :
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that judgment will produce — the people will know their situation, though they are currently boasting in arrogance.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:9 Who among all these does not know that the hand of the Lord has done this ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's action that creation itself attests — a knowing available to all who observe.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:19 You say, ‘ God stores up their iniquity for their children .’ Let him pay it out to them, that they may know it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the experiential knowing of God's judgment — recompense producing inner recognition of justice.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:7 He seals up the hand of every man , that all men whom he made may know it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the divine purpose in sealing human hands in winter — all created persons are to know God's sovereign power through it.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:24 You shall know that your tent is at peace , and you shall inspect your fold and miss nothing .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's blessing — the restored person will know peace and security by experience.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:25 You shall know also that your offspring shall be many , and your descendants as the grass of the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's fruitfulness — blessing known through lived experience.

verses.verse_text: Judg 18:5 And they said to him, “ Inquire of God , please, that we may know whether the journey on which we are setting out will succeed .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names seeking divine disclosure about the future — asking God to make known his will for the journey.

verses.verse_text: Judg 6:37 behold , I am laying a fleece of wool on the threshing floor . If there is dew on the fleece alone , and it is dry on all the ground , then I shall know that you will save Israel by my hand , as you have said .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Gideon's seeking of assurance of God's covenantal commitment — the sign will produce inner certainty.

verses.verse_text: Mic 6:5 O my people , remember what Balak king of Moab devised , and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him, and what happened from Shittim to Gilgal , that you may know the righteous acts of the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's saving history that remembrance produces — recalling Balak and Balaam produces recognition of God's faithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Num 12:6 And he said , “ Hear my words : If there is a prophet among you, I the Lord make myself known to him in a vision ; I speak with him in a dream .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's self-disclosure to prophets — revelatory knowing through vision and dream. The mode of God making himself known.

verses.verse_text: Num 14:31 But your little ones , who you said would become a prey , I will bring in , and they shall know the land that you have rejected .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the experiential covenantal knowing of the land — the children will know it by inhabiting it, which the faithless generation refused.

verses.verse_text: Num 14:34 According to the number of the days in which you spied out the land , forty days , a year for each day , you shall bear your iniquity forty years , and you shall know my displeasure .’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the experiential knowledge of God's judgment — the forty years will produce knowing of what it means to have God against them.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:28 And Moses said , “ Hereby you shall know that the Lord has sent me to do all these works , and that it has not been of my own accord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of divine authority that the sign will produce — covenantal self-authentication through event.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:30 But if the Lord creates something new , and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them up with all that belongs to them, and they go down alive into Sheol , then you shall know that these men have despised the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that the judgment will produce — the event makes the inner spiritual reality visible and knowable.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:5 and he said to Korah and all his company , “ In the morning the Lord will show who is his, and who is holy , and will bring him near to him. The one whom he chooses he will bring near to him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names divine self-disclosure through vindicating action — God will make known who belongs to him.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:19 So you, too , please stay here tonight , that I may know what more the Lord will say to me .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Balaam's seeking of divine disclosure — waiting for God to make his will known.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:2 and the sign or wonder that he tells you comes to pass , and if he says , ‘Let us go after other gods ,’ which you have not known , ‘and let us serve them ,’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational absence — the false gods have no covenant with Israel, no history of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:3 you shall not listen to the words of that prophet or that dreamer of dreams . For the Lord your God is testing you, to know whether you love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that the test produces — the inner orientation of love is made visible through the test.

verses.verse_text: Deu 18:21 And if you say in your heart , ‘ How may we know the word that the Lord has not spoken ?’—
verses.meaning: ya.da names the discernment of authentic divine disclosure — recognising what is genuinely from God.

verses.verse_text: Deu 2:7 For the Lord your God has blessed you in all the work of your hands . He knows your going through this great wilderness . These forty years the Lord your God has been with you. You have lacked nothing .”’
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal attending — he knows the journey of his people with them.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:4 But to this day the Lord has not given you a heart to understand or eyes to see or ears to hear .
verses.meaning: ya.da names covenantal knowing as a divine gift withheld — the heart to understand/know must be given; it is not naturally present.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:35 To you it was shown , that you might know that the Lord is God ; there is no other besides him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the purpose of divine self-disclosure — the events of the Exodus were so that Israel would know God's uniqueness. Covenantal knowing through experience.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:16 who fed you in the wilderness with manna that your fathers did not know , that he might humble you and test you, to do you good in the end .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's purpose in the manna — the testing produces knowing of dependence and produces good.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:2 And you shall remember the whole way that the Lord your God has led you these forty years in the wilderness , that he might humble you, testing you to know what was in your heart , whether you would keep his commandments or not .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge God seeks through the test — not absent knowledge but the process of making the heart's character visible.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:3 And he humbled you and let you hunger and fed you with manna , which you did not know , nor did your fathers know , that he might make you know that man does not live by bread alone , but man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's pedagogical disclosure through the manna — the humbling and feeding produce inner knowledge of dependence on God's word.

verses.verse_text: Exo 3:7 Then the Lord said , “I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters . I know their sufferings ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive inner knowledge of Israel's affliction — not distant observation but intimate knowing of their condition.

verses.verse_text: Exo 4:14 Then the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses and he said , “Is there not Aaron , your brother , the Levite ? I know that he can speak well . Behold, he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you, he will be glad in his heart .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Aaron's capacity — inner knowledge of another person's gift.

verses.verse_text: Jos 3:4 Yet there shall be a distance between you and it, about 2,000 cubits in length . Do not come near it, in order that you may know the way you shall go , for you have not passed this way before .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's leading that following the ark will produce — a new path made known through divine guidance.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:23 If you turn at my reproof , behold , I will pour out my spirit to you; I will make my words known to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Wisdom's self-disclosure — she will reveal her words to those who turn at her reproof. Covenantal disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:19 That your trust may be in the Lord , I have made them known to you today , even to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the teacher's disclosure — making known the thirty sayings as a covenantal transmission of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Zec 11:11 So it was annulled on that day , and the sheep traders , who were watching me, knew that it was the word of the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine speech — observers perceive that the act is the word of the Lord.

verse_context_groups.label: Human covenantal knowing of God
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the human person's relational inner knowing of God — pressing on to know him, finding the knowledge of God, knowing him in the act of doing justice. This is the human side of the covenantal encounter: the person's inner recognition of who God is, sustained by covenant practice. Inner-being phenomenon: human covenantal knowledge of God.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Hos 6:3 Let us know ; let us press on to know the Lord ; his going out is sure as the dawn ; he will come to us as the showers , as the spring rains that water the earth .”
anchor_verse.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of the Lord as the goal of urgent pursuit — pressing on to know God as a relational aspiration.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'Let us know; let us press on to know the Lord; his going out is sure as the dawn.' The most direct statement of human covenantal pursuit of knowing God. The verb is active — pressing on, seeking — and the object is the Lord himself as a relational reality. The simile (dawn, spring rain) names the certainty and fruitfulness of what this inner pursuit will encounter.

verses.verse_text: Hos 6:3 Let us know ; let us press on to know the Lord ; his going out is sure as the dawn ; he will come to us as the showers , as the spring rains that water the earth .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of the Lord as the goal of urgent pursuit — pressing on to know God as a relational aspiration.

verses.verse_text: Hos 2:20 I will betroth you to me in faithfulness . And you shall know the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the restored covenantal knowing as the goal of betrothal — the covenant renewed produces the knowing of the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:8 But he said , “O Lord God , how am I to know that I shall possess it ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Abram's seeking of covenantal assurance — a request for knowledge of God's promise that goes beyond verbal affirmation.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:4 But I am the Lord your God from the land of Egypt ; you know no God but me, and besides me there is no savior .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the exclusive covenantal knowing — Israel's only knowing of God is in relation to the one who delivered them from Egypt.

verses.verse_text: Hos 6:6 For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice , the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God as what God desires above sacrifice — relational inner knowing as the substance of true worship. Note: assigned to 1838-001, but this is a positive statement of what God desires, not culpable ignorance. The assignment appears to be a misrouting.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:2 “ Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge ?
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner substance God charges Job with lacking — his words darkened rather than illuminated counsel.

verses.verse_text: Job 42:3 ‘ Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge ?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand , things too wonderful for me, which I did not know .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge Job lacked — he spoke beyond his inner knowledge. The repentance acknowledges the limit.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:2 What you know , I also know; I am not inferior to you .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge held by Job and his friends — inner intellectual and experiential knowledge as the ground of dispute.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:2 “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge , and fill his belly with the east wind?
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge emptied of substance — Eliphaz accuses Job of speaking words without real inner content.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:6 In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths .
verses.meaning: ya.da names acknowledgment of God as the relational covenantal orienting — the knowing that shapes the whole direction of life.

verses.verse_text: Hos 8:2 To me they cry , “My God , we— Israel — know you.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Israel's claimed covenantal knowing — a claim made while pursuing other gods. The knowing they claim is hollow.

verses.verse_text: Isa 51:7 “ Listen to me, you who know righteousness , the people in whose heart is my law ; fear not the reproach of man , nor be dismayed at their revilings .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of righteousness — God's law in the heart is its content.

verses.verse_text: Gen 30:26 Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, that I may go , for you know the service that I have given you .”
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged inner awareness — the term identifies Laban's own knowledge of Jacob's service as a fact Jacob appeals to in requesting release.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:13 and that their children , who have not known it, may hear and learn to fear the Lord your God , as long as you live in the land that you are going over the Jordan to possess .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that the new generation lacks and must receive — not yet knowing the Lord as their parents did.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:5 then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God as the goal of seeking wisdom — not mere information but the defining inner orientation toward God.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom , and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of the Holy One as what constitutes insight — knowing God is not an add-on but the substance of understanding.

verses.verse_text: Isa 50:4 The Lord God has given me the tongue of those who are taught , that I may know how to sustain with a word him who is weary . Morning by morning he awakens ; he awakens my ear to hear as those who are taught .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing the Servant receives morning by morning — the knowing of how to speak life into the weary.

verses.verse_text: Jer 22:16 He judged the cause of the poor and needy ; then it was well . Is not this to know me? declares the Lord .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God as expressed in justice for the vulnerable — knowing God and doing justice are equated. A profound definitional statement.

verses.verse_text: Jos 2:9 and said to the men , “I know that the Lord has given you the land , and that the fear of you has fallen upon us, and that all the inhabitants of the land melt away before you.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Rahab's inner covenantal recognition — she knows what God has done and what it means for the city. Knowledge producing allegiance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:21 Do you not know ? Do you not hear ? Has it not been told you from the beginning ? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive reception of what has been available from the beginning — the question challenges those who act as if they do not know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:10 “ You are my witnesses ,” declares the Lord , “and my servant whom I have chosen , that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed , nor shall there be any after me.
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive reception of God's identity — understanding that God is who he is, as the goal of Israel's witness role.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:11 Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied ; by his knowledge shall the righteous one , my servant , make many to be accounted righteous , and he shall bear their iniquities .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge by which the Servant justifies — his inner knowing is the instrument of the many's righteousness. A remarkable and distinctive use.

verses.verse_text: Num 24:16 the oracle of him who hears the words of God , and knows the knowledge of the Most High , who sees the vision of the Almighty , falling down with his eyes uncovered :
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of the Most High as an inner capacity of the seer — divine knowledge held within the person as a gift. Note: assigned to 1838-001; however this is a positive description of the seer's inner capacity, not culpable ignorance. The assignment appears to be a misrouting.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph , “ Since God has shown you all this , there is none so discerning and wise as you are.
verses.meaning: ya.da names divine disclosure — God has shown Joseph, and this showing has produced knowledge beyond human capacity.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:24 And those who go astray in spirit will come to understanding , and those who murmur will accept instruction .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God that restoration produces — the erring will come to know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:4 The heart of the hasty will understand and know , and the tongue of the stammerers will hasten to speak distinctly .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowing that will come in the renewal — the hasty heart will finally perceive rightly.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:21 Do you not know ? Do you not hear ? Has it not been told you from the beginning ? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge that should have been received from the beginning — Israel has had access to this knowing through tradition.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:28 Have you not known ? Have you not heard ? The Lord is the everlasting God , the Creator of the ends of the earth . He does not faint or grow weary ; his understanding is unsearchable .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's eternal nature — what should be known by his people.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:10 “ You are my witnesses ,” declares the Lord , “and my servant whom I have chosen , that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed , nor shall there be any after me.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's identity as the goal of Israel's witness — knowing that God is who he is, uniquely.

verses.verse_text: Job 10:2 I will say to God , Do not condemn me; let me know why you contend against me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's request for knowledge of God's purposes — seeking inner comprehension of his situation.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:23 How many are my iniquities and my sins ? Make me know my transgression and my sin .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's request for inner moral knowledge of what he has done wrong — seeking self-disclosure through God's disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:3 Oh , that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his seat !
verses.meaning: ya.da names the longed-for covenantal knowing of God's location — Job's agonised desire to encounter the God who seems hidden.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:5 I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge Job seeks — hearing God's answer as the goal of his seeking.

verses.verse_text: Mic 3:1 And I said : Hear , you heads of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel ! Is it not for you to know justice ?—
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of justice as the obligation of leaders — they should know it; their failure to practise it is not ignorance but refusal.

verses.verse_text: Neh 10:28 “The rest of the people , the priests , the Levites , the gatekeepers , the singers , the temple servants , and all who have separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the Law of God , their wives , their sons , their daughters , all who have knowledge and understanding ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names knowledge as a defining inner quality of those who separate themselves to the Law — the covenant community characterised by knowing.

verses.verse_text: Num 24:16 the oracle of him who hears the words of God , and knows the knowledge of the Most High , who sees the vision of the Almighty , falling down with his eyes uncovered :
verses.meaning: ya.da names the seer's inner knowing of divine knowledge — same verse, same observation about the misassignment.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:5 Evil men do not understand justice , but those who seek the Lord understand it completely .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty opened by seeking God — those who seek the Lord understand justice in full.

verses.verse_text: Deu 11:2 And consider today ( since I am not speaking to your children who have not known or seen it), consider the discipline of the Lord your God , his greatness , his mighty hand and his outstretched arm ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the remembering and considering that keeps covenantal knowledge alive — the mighty acts known experientially.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:39 know therefore today , and lay it to your heart , that the Lord is God in heaven above and on the earth beneath ; there is no other .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner appropriation of covenantal truth — knowing laid to the heart as the act of receiving divine reality inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Deu 7:9 Know therefore that the Lord your God is God , the faithful God who keeps covenant and steadfast love with those who love him and keep his commandments , to a thousand generations ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names covenantal knowledge of God's character — the command to know is a call to inner recognition of who God is.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:5 Know then in your heart that, as a man disciplines his son , the Lord your God disciplines you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner appropriation of understanding God's discipline — knowing in the heart as covenantal receiving.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:3 Know therefore today that he who goes over before you as a consuming fire is the Lord your God . He will destroy them and subdue them before you. So you shall drive them out and make them perish quickly , as the Lord has promised you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge being commanded — inner recognition of God as the one fighting for Israel.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:6 “ Know , therefore, that the Lord your God is not giving you this good land to possess because of your righteousness , for you are a stubborn people .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the corrective knowledge Israel must hold — inner recognition that grace, not merit, is the ground of the gift.

verses.verse_text: Jon 4:2 And he prayed to the Lord and said , “ O Lord , is not this what I said when I was yet in my country ? That is why I made haste to flee to Tarshish ; for I knew that you are a gracious God and merciful , slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love , and relenting from disaster .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Jonah's covenantal knowledge of God's character — the knowing that motivated his flight.

verses.verse_text: Jos 14:6 Then the people of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal . And Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him, “ You know what the Lord said to Moses the man of God in Kadesh-barnea concerning you and me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of a divine word given in the past — Caleb appeals to Joshua's shared covenantal memory.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:10 Whoever is righteous has regard for the life of his beast , but the mercy of the wicked is cruel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowing expressed in care — the righteous person's inner knowledge extends to regard for the animal.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:14 Know that wisdom is such to your soul ; if you find it, there will be a future , and your hope will not be cut off .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal inner knowing of wisdom's value — held in the soul as certain hope.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of justice — the righteous person's inner knowing extends to the claims of the vulnerable.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:4 Who has ascended to heaven and come down ? Who has gathered the wind in his fists ? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment ? Who has established all the ends of the earth ? What is his name , and what is his son’s name ? Surely you know !
verses.meaning: Names the ironic inner competence the challenge demands — the term identifies knowing the name of the one who ordered creation as the knowledge required to answer.

verse_context_groups.label: Covenantal absence: not knowing God
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the structural opposite — a person, community, or nation that does not know God, refuses the knowledge of God, or lacks it. The sons of Eli did not know the Lord. Israel has no knowledge of God in the land. The wicked do not desire the knowledge of his ways. Sometimes the absence is culpable refusal; sometimes it is the condition of those outside the covenant. Inner-being phenomenon: absent or refused covenantal knowing.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Hos 4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge ; because you have rejected knowledge , I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the law of your God , I also will forget your children .
anchor_verse.meaning: da.at names knowledge of God as the inner orientation whose rejection produces destruction — the most explicit statement of the consequences of da.at's absence.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge; because you have rejected knowledge, I reject you from being a priest to me.' The most explicit statement of covenantal ignorance's consequence: destruction, priestly rejection, divine forgetting of the next generation. The verse names the absence (lack of knowledge), the act behind it (rejection), and the catastrophic result. The full weight of not knowing God is stated here.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 2:12 Now the sons of Eli were worthless men . They did not know the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal relationship — Eli's sons had no inner knowing of the Lord, which is why they profaned the offering.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:7 Now Samuel did not yet know the Lord , and the word of the Lord had not yet been revealed to him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that had not yet begun — the knowing of God is tied to the receiving of his word.

verses.verse_text: Judg 2:10 And all that generation also were gathered to their fathers . And there arose another generation after them who did not know the Lord or the work that he had done for Israel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing broken between generations — the new generation has no experiential or transmitted knowledge of God.

verses.verse_text: Hos 4:1 Hear the word of the Lord , O children of Israel , for the Lord has a controversy with the inhabitants of the land . There is no faithfulness or steadfast love , and no knowledge of God in the land ;
verses.meaning: da.at names the defining absence in Israel — without the knowledge of God, the land has no faithfulness or steadfast love. All three are interlinked.

verses.verse_text: Hos 4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge ; because you have rejected knowledge , I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the law of your God , I also will forget your children .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of God as the inner orientation whose rejection produces destruction — the most explicit statement of the consequences of da.at's absence.

verses.verse_text: Job 35:16 Job opens his mouth in empty talk ; he multiplies words without knowledge .”
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner substance absent in Job's words — words without real knowledge behind them.

verses.verse_text: Hos 11:3 Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk; I took them up by their arms , but they did not know that I healed them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal recognition of God's care — the father taught and healed, but was not known as the one doing it.

verses.verse_text: Isa 56:10 His watchmen are blind ; they are all without knowledge ; they are all silent dogs ; they cannot bark , dreaming , lying down , loving to slumber .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Israel's watchmen as those who are all without knowledge, completely unable to perceive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:13 Therefore my people go into exile for lack of knowledge ; their honored men go hungry , and their multitude is parched with thirst .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of God as what Israel lacks — the absence produces exile. The knowledge of God and national stability are directly linked.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:10 You felt secure in your wickedness ; you said , “No one sees me”; your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray , and you said in your heart , “ I am, and there is no one besides me.”
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge that has become the instrument of self-deception — Babylon's inner knowing has turned against her.

verses.verse_text: Isa 58:2 Yet they seek me daily and delight to know my ways , as if they were a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the judgment of their God ; they ask of me righteous judgments ; they delight to draw near to God .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God's ways as what the hypocrites claim to delight in — the inner orientation they perform without genuinely holding.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:14 Every man is stupid and without knowledge ; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols , for his images are false , and there is no breath in them .
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner capacity absent in idol-makers — without it, their craft produces only falsehood.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:17 Every man is stupid and without knowledge ; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols , for his images are false , and there is no breath in them .
verses.meaning: da.at names the absent inner capacity — same as Jer 10:14.

verses.verse_text: Hos 5:4 Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God . For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they know not the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal knowing — the spirit of whoredom has displaced knowing of the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Pro 27:23 Know well the condition of your flocks , and give attention to your herds ,
verses.meaning: Names the directed inner attention — the term identifies knowing well the condition of one's flocks as the practical wisdom required of a shepherd.

verses.verse_text: Jer 3:15 “‘And I will give you shepherds after my own heart , who will feed you with knowledge and understanding .
verses.meaning: de.ah names knowledge as what the coming shepherds will give — inner content of formation, alongside understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:3 I have not learned wisdom , nor have I knowledge of the Holy One .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of the Holy One as what Agur disclaims — the inner orientation toward God he does not possess.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:29 Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the rejected call to wisdom offered — it was available and refused.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:19 No one considers , nor is there knowledge or discernment to say , “ Half of it I burned in the fire ; I also baked bread on its coals ; I roasted meat and have eaten . And shall I make the rest of it an abomination ? Shall I fall down before a block of wood ?”
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names discernment as the inner capacity that idol-worshippers lack — unable to perceive the absurdity of their practice.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:20 “Assemble yourselves and come ; draw near together , you survivors of the nations ! They have no knowledge who carry about their wooden idols , and keep on praying to a god that cannot save .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowing of idol-carriers — the one who prays to a god that cannot save has no inner knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:19 No one considers , nor is there knowledge or discernment to say , “ Half of it I burned in the fire ; I also baked bread on its coals ; I roasted meat and have eaten . And shall I make the rest of it an abomination ? Shall I fall down before a block of wood ?”
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge absent in the idol-worshipper — they lack the inner capacity to perceive the absurdity of their practice.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:25 who frustrates the signs of liars and makes fools of diviners , who turns wise men back and makes their knowledge foolish ,
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge God frustrates — the supposed inner knowing of diviners and wise men rendered empty.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:14 They say to God , ‘ Depart from us! We do not desire the knowledge of your ways .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God's ways as what the wicked refuse — the inner orientation toward God that they actively reject.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:12 But if they do not listen , they perish by the sword and die without knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner orientation toward God whose absence leads to ruin — dying without knowledge is dying without having known God's ways.

verses.verse_text: Hos 2:8 And she did not know that it was I who gave her the grain , the wine , and the oil , and who lavished on her silver and gold , which they used for Baal .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal recognition of God as provider — Israel received but did not know the source.

verses.verse_text: Hos 7:9 Strangers devour his strength , and he knows it not; gray hairs are sprinkled upon him, and he knows it not.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent self-knowledge of Ephraim — he cannot perceive his own decline.

verses.verse_text: Hos 8:4 They made kings , but not through me. They set up princes , but I knew it not. With their silver and gold they made idols for their own destruction .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's non-acknowledgment — he did not know it, meaning he did not sanction or recognise it.

verses.verse_text: Isa 1:3 The ox knows its owner , and the donkey its master’s crib , but Israel does not know , my people do not understand .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing Israel lacks — even animals recognise their master; Israel does not recognise God.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:15 Ah , you who hide deep from the Lord your counsel , whose deeds are in the dark , and who say , “ Who sees us? Who knows us ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the divine knowing they deny — those who hide from God claim he cannot know their deeds, which is precisely what God knows.

verses.verse_text: Isa 42:25 So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the might of battle ; it set him on fire all around , but he did not understand ; it burned him up, but he did not take it to heart .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner comprehension — the term identifies the failure to understand or take to heart the consuming fire of divine wrath as a form of inner blindness.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:9 All who fashion idols are nothing , and the things they delight in do not profit . Their witnesses neither see nor know , that they may be put to shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral blindness of idol-witnesses — they cannot perceive or know the truth.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:4 For the sake of my servant Jacob , and Israel my chosen , I call you by your name , I name you, though you do not know me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing Cyrus lacks — God calls him by name without the relationship of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:5 I am the Lord , and there is no other , besides me there is no God ; I equip you, though you do not know me ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same absence of covenantal knowing — Cyrus is equipped without the relationship.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:11 But evil shall come upon you, which you will not know how to charm away ; disaster shall fall upon you, for which you will not be able to atone ; and ruin shall come upon you suddenly , of which you know nothing .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent inner knowledge of how to avert disaster — Babylon will be helpless.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:8 Now therefore hear this , you lover of pleasures , who sit securely , who say in your heart , “ I am, and there is no one besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children ”:
verses.meaning: ya.da names Babylon's self-deceiving inner certainty — she claims she will not experience what she in fact will experience.

verses.verse_text: Isa 58:3 ‘ Why have we fasted , and you see it not ? Why have we humbled ourselves , and you take no knowledge of it?’ Behold , in the day of your fast you seek your own pleasure , and oppress all your workers .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's attending — the people claim God does not perceive their fast. The accusation is about divine knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 59:8 The way of peace they do not know , and there is no justice in their paths ; they have made their roads crooked ; no one who treads on them knows peace .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowledge of the unjust — they cannot perceive the path of peace.

verses.verse_text: Job 18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the unrighteous , such is the place of him who knows not God .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal knowing of God as the defining characteristic of the wicked — their ruin is identified by what they do not know.

verses.verse_text: Job 24:16 In the dark they dig through houses ; by day they shut themselves up ; they do not know the light .
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowledge absent in the wicked — they do not know the light; their moral orientation is darkness.

verses.verse_text: Judg 16:20 And she said , “The Philistines are upon you, Samson !” And he awoke from his sleep and said , “I will go out as at other times and shake myself free .” But he did not know that the Lord had left him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal absence that Samson is unaware of — the Lord's departure is an inner spiritual reality he cannot perceive.

verses.verse_text: Amo 3:10 “They do not know how to do right ,” declares the Lord , “those who store up violence and robbery in their strongholds .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowledge of the unjust — they lack the inner knowing of what right conduct is.

verses.verse_text: Deu 11:28 and the curse , if you do not obey the commandments of the Lord your God , but turn aside from the way that I am commanding you today , to go after other gods that you have not known .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal relationship — gods that Israel has no knowing with, in contrast to the Lord they do know.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:13 that certain worthless fellows have gone out among you and have drawn away the inhabitants of their city , saying , ‘Let us go and serve other gods ,’ which you have not known ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same covenantal absence — these gods have no claim on Israel through knowing.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:26 and went and served other gods and worshiped them, gods whom they had not known and whom he had not allotted to them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational absence — these gods have no covenant history with Israel; they have not been known or given.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:17 They sacrificed to demons that were not God , to gods they had never known , to new gods that had come recently , whom your fathers had never dreaded .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational absence with the new gods — no history of knowing, no covenant, no claim.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God . To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools , for they do not know that they are doing evil .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent inner moral knowledge of the thoughtless worshipper — they cannot perceive their own wrongdoing.
