verses.verse_text: Exo 5:2 But Pharaoh said , “Who is the Lord , that I should obey his voice and let Israel go ? I do not know the Lord , and moreover , I will not let Israel go .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Pharaoh's refusal of covenantal knowing — his denial of the Lord is both ignorance and defiance.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:3 I have not learned wisdom , nor have I knowledge of the Holy One .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of the Holy One that Agur lacks — relational inner knowing of God.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:13 The woman Folly is loud ; she is seductive and knows nothing .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the total inner ignorance of folly — she has no knowledge; her noise is empty.

verses.verse_text: Zep 3:5 The Lord within her is righteous ; he does no injustice ; every morning he shows forth his justice ; each dawn he does not fail ; but the unjust knows no shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral self-knowledge of the unjust — they cannot perceive their own wrongdoing or feel shame for it.

verse_context_groups.label: da.at as inner content and capacity held by the person
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names knowledge as an inner content or capacity possessed, accumulated, guarded, and transmitted — the Proverbs portrait of da.at as a valued inner endowment. The wise lay up knowledge; lips of the wise spread it; knowledge is pleasant to the soul; the prudent guard it. Includes knowledge as the content of wise speech, as God's creative-ordering capacity, and as the transmissible substance of wisdom instruction. Inner-being phenomenon: knowledge as inner content held and exercised.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Pro 1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge ; fools despise wisdom and instruction .
anchor_verse.meaning: da.at names knowledge as having its ground in the fear of the Lord — the inner orientation toward God is the foundation of all genuine knowing.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.' Names knowledge as having a covenantal ground (fear of the Lord), a positive possessor (those who begin with this), and a structural opponent (fools who despise it). The whole Proverbs portrait of da.at as constituted inner capacity is implied in this programmatic statement.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:4 to give prudence to the simple , knowledge and discretion to the youth —
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what Proverbs aims to give the young — inner capacity paired with prudence and discretion as formative endowments.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge ; fools despise wisdom and instruction .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as having its ground in the fear of the Lord — the inner orientation toward God is the foundation of all genuine knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:22 “How long , O simple ones , will you love being simple ? How long will scoffers delight in their scoffing and fools hate knowledge ?
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what fools actively reject — the inner capacity and orientation is an object of hatred for those turned from wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom ; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding ;
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a divine gift from God's mouth — an inner endowment transmitted through word.

verses.verse_text: Pro 5:2 that you may keep discretion , and your lips may guard knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something held and guarded — an inner possession that must be kept.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:9 They are all straight to him who understands , and right to those who find knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what makes wisdom's words accessible — those who have the inner capacity find them straight.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:10 Take my instruction instead of silver , and knowledge rather than choice gold ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as more valuable than gold — its worth is inner, not material.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:12 “ I , wisdom , dwell with prudence , and I find knowledge and discretion .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what Wisdom holds within herself — an inner possession she finds and offers.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:14 The wise lay up knowledge , but the mouth of a fool brings ruin near .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something accumulated and stored inwardly by the wise — an inner treasury.

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:9 With his mouth the godless man would destroy his neighbor , but by knowledge the righteous are delivered .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a protective inner capacity — what delivers the righteous from the godless person's destruction.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:1 Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge , but he who hates reproof is stupid .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what discipline produces — the person who receives correction is on the path of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:23 A prudent man conceals knowledge , but the heart of fools proclaims folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something the prudent hold inwardly rather than displaying — restraint is a sign of genuine inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:16 Every prudent man acts with knowledge , but a fool flaunts his folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what underlies prudent action — the inner capacity expressed in conduct.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:6 A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain , but knowledge is easy for a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as accessible to those who have understanding — the inner faculty opens the way to knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:7 Leave the presence of a fool , for there you do not meet words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as absent in the fool's speech — leaving the fool's presence means leaving where knowledge is not found.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:18 The simple inherit folly , but the prudent are crowned with knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the crown of the prudent — what adorns and marks the person of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:2 The tongue of the wise commends knowledge , but the mouths of fools pour out folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what wise speech honours — the inner capacity expressed through commending words.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:7 The lips of the wise spread knowledge ; not so the hearts of fools .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the wise disseminate — the inner capacity communicated through speech.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:14 The heart of him who has understanding seeks knowledge , but the mouths of fools feed on folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the object of the understanding heart's seeking — an inner orientation toward further knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:27 Whoever restrains his words has knowledge , and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what underlies restraint — inner knowing expressed in the discipline of speech.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:15 An intelligent heart acquires knowledge , and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the intelligent heart acquires and the wise ear seeks — active inner pursuit of the capacity.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:2 Desire without knowledge is not good , and whoever makes haste with his feet misses his way .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what must accompany desire for it to be good — inner knowing directs desire; without it, haste leads astray.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:25 Strike a scoffer , and the simple will learn prudence ; reprove a man of understanding , and he will gain knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge produced by receiving correction — the person of understanding grows in inner capacity through rebuke.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:27 Cease to hear instruction , my son , and you will stray from the words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the content of instructive words — ceasing to hear instruction means leaving the sphere of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 20:15 There is gold and abundance of costly stones , but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a precious inner possession expressed through speech — rare and valuable.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:11 When a scoffer is punished , the simple becomes wise ; when a wise man is instructed , he gains knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the fruit of instruction for the wise — the inner capacity grows with receiving.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:12 The eyes of the Lord keep watch over knowledge , but he overthrows the words of the traitor .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what God guards — he watches over the inner capacity and protects it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:17 Incline your ear , and hear the words of the wise , and apply your heart to my knowledge ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the heart must be applied to — inner orientation of the heart toward received wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:20 Have I not written for you thirty sayings of counsel and knowledge ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the content of the sayings — the collection is characterised by its inner substance.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:12 Apply your heart to instruction and your ear to words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what words of instruction carry — the ear applied to receive their inner content.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:16 I said in my heart , “ I have acquired great wisdom , surpassing all who were over Jerusalem before me, and my heart has had great experience of wisdom and knowledge .”
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as an inner experiential possession — the heart's engagement with knowing as part of the quest.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:18 For in much wisdom is much vexation , and he who increases knowledge increases sorrow .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something that brings inner pain — the more one knows, the more the limits and griefs of existence become apparent.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:21 because sometimes a person who has toiled with wisdom and knowledge and skill must leave everything to be enjoyed by someone who did not toil for it. This also is vanity and a great evil .
verses.meaning: Names the intellectual effort of the one who toiled — the term identifies knowledge as a valued inner quality that the toiler brought to his labour, only for its fruit to pass to another.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:26 For to the one who pleases him God has given wisdom and knowledge and joy , but to the sinner he has given the business of gathering and collecting , only to give to one who pleases God . This also is vanity and a striving after wind .
verses.meaning: Names the capacity God gives to those who please him — the term identifies knowledge as a gift bestowed alongside wisdom and joy, in contrast to the sinner's mere accumulation.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money , and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.
verses.meaning: Names the cognitive advantage that wisdom carries — the term identifies knowledge as preserving the life of the one who possesses it, offering an advantage beyond material protection.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do , do it with your might , for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol , to which you are going .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as belonging to the living — it ceases at death. Inner knowing is a capacity of the living person.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 12:9 Besides being wise , the Preacher also taught the people knowledge , weighing and studying and arranging many proverbs with great care.
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the Preacher transmitted — the inner capacity imparted through the collected proverbs.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:6 and he will be the stability of your times , abundance of salvation , wisdom , and knowledge ; the fear of the Lord is Zion’s treasure .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as part of God's gift of stability — Spirit-given inner knowing as part of the coming abundance.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as one of the inner qualities God gave the youths — a Spirit-given capacity alongside understanding and learning.

verses.verse_text: Dan 12:4 But you , Daniel , shut up the words and seal the book , until the time of the end . Many shall run to and fro , and knowledge shall increase .”
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what will increase in the time of the end — an eschatological expansion of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:9 “To whom will he teach knowledge , and to whom will he explain the message ? Those who are weaned from the milk , those taken from the breast ?
verses.meaning: de.ah names knowledge as what the teacher would impart — the inner content of instruction. The scoffers mock the simplicity of the message.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:6 And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said : “I am young in years , and you are aged ; therefore I was timid and afraid to declare my opinion to you .
verses.meaning: de.a names the inner formed judgment or opinion held inwardly — Elihu has withheld his inner view out of deference to age.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:10 Therefore I say , ‘ Listen to me; let me also declare my opinion .’
verses.meaning: de.a names the inner formed opinion now to be expressed — Elihu's inner position held and now released.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:17 I also will answer with my share ; I also will declare my opinion .
verses.meaning: de.a names the same — the inner formed view ready for expression.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:3 I will get my knowledge from afar and ascribe righteousness to my Maker .
verses.meaning: de.a names Elihu's claim to knowledge oriented toward God — inner formed understanding directed away from self and toward God.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:12 because an excellent spirit , knowledge , and understanding to interpret dreams , explain riddles , and solve problems were found in this Daniel , whom the king named Belteshazzar . Now let Daniel be called , and he will show the interpretation .”
verses.meaning: man.da names knowledge as part of Daniel's Spirit-given capacity — alongside understanding, enabling interpretation of mysteries.

verses.verse_text: Mal 2:7 For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge , and people should seek instruction from his mouth , for he is the messenger of the Lord of hosts .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the priestly possession that must be guarded and dispensed — the inner capacity held by the priest for the benefit of the community.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:66 Teach me good judgment and knowledge , for I believe in your commandments .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the psalmist seeks from God — inner capacity oriented toward the commandments, sought in prayer.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:2 The tongue of the wise commends knowledge , but the mouths of fools pour out folly .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality whose expression in speech honours and promotes knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:7 The lips of the wise spread knowledge ; not so the hearts of fools .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose speech disseminates knowledge — wisdom communicates what it holds inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:17 Incline your ear , and hear the words of the wise , and apply your heart to my knowledge ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose words deserve to be heard — their inner quality makes their speech authoritative.

verses.verse_text: Isa 11:2 And the Spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding , the Spirit of counsel and might , the Spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a Spirit-given endowment on the Messiah — one of the sevenfold gifts resting on him.

verses.verse_text: Jos 20:5 And if the avenger of blood pursues him, they shall not give up the manslayer into his hand , because he struck his neighbor unknowingly , and did not hate him in the past .
verses.meaning: da.at names the absence of inner enmity — the act was not driven by the moral knowledge of hatred.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as one of the inner qualities the youths possess — alongside knowledge and learning, qualifying them for royal service.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:9 “To whom will he teach knowledge , and to whom will he explain the message ? Those who are weaned from the milk , those taken from the breast ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty needed to receive explanation — the question implies the audience lacks the capacity to receive.

verses.verse_text: Job 33:3 My words declare the uprightness of my heart , and what my lips know they speak sincerely .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that the lips hold — inner knowledge expressed outwardly in sincere speech.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:35 ‘ Job speaks without knowledge ; his words are without insight .’
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge Elihu says Job's speech lacks — inner content of substance. Empty speech is speech without da.at.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:10 for wisdom will come into your heart , and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul ;
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something experienced inwardly as delight — it enters the soul as pleasure, not as burden. The inner-being resonance is direct.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:4 by knowledge the rooms are filled with all precious and pleasant riches .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what produces abundance — the inner capacity fills what wisdom builds.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:5 A wise man is full of strength , and a man of knowledge enhances his might ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what augments strength — inner knowing makes the person more effective.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the inner capacity that moral orientation blocks — the wicked cannot receive it.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:17 And I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly . I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the deliberate inner pursuit of knowing — turning the heart toward wisdom and its opposites as a quest for self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom , and to see the business that is done on earth , how neither day nor night do one’s eyes see sleep ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the deliberate inner turning toward knowing — the heart applied as a means of pursuit.

verse_context_groups.label: Knowledge of good and evil (moral-discriminating knowing)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the specifically moral knowing that distinguishes good from evil — named in the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, acquired through the fall, withheld from moral infants, and called for as a developmental marker. This is knowing as a moral-discriminating capacity: the person who can distinguish right from wrong, choose the good, refuse the evil. Inner-being phenomenon: moral discriminating knowledge.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Gen 2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat , for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die .”
anchor_verse.meaning: da.at names the knowledge God withholds from the human — moral self-determination, the capacity to define good and evil for oneself. The prohibition frames this knowledge as dangerous.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.' The naming of the prohibited inner capacity. The tree is defined by what it contains: knowledge of good and evil as a transformative moral orientation. The prohibition reveals what is at stake — this knowledge is not information but a capacity that will reshape the inner person, with consequences.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:22 Then the Lord God said , “ Behold , the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil . Now , lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat , and live forever —”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge now acquired — knowing good and evil as a divine-level capacity. The consequence is permanent inner transformation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 2:9 And out of the ground the Lord God made to spring up every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food . The tree of life was in the midst of the garden , and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that the tree embodies — moral knowledge of good and evil as a defining inner capacity. The tree is named by what it contains; knowledge of good and evil is not mere information but a transformative inner orientation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat , for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die .”
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge God withholds from the human — moral self-determination, the capacity to define good and evil for oneself. The prohibition frames this knowledge as dangerous.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:5 For God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened , and you will be like God , knowing good and evil .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names what the serpent attributes to God — divine self-knowledge of what the fruit does. The temptation is access to what God knows.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:7 Then the eyes of both were opened , and they knew that they were naked . And they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves loincloths .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the immediate experiential moral knowledge produced by eating — self-awareness of nakedness as the first fruit of the transgression.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:15 He shall eat curds and honey when he knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowing of good and evil — a developmental inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:16 For before the boy knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good , the land whose two kings you dread will be deserted .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same moral knowing as a developmental marker — before it is reached, the sign will be fulfilled.

verses.verse_text: Isa 8:4 for before the boy knows how to cry ‘My father ’ or ‘ My mother ,’ the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria will be carried away before the king of Assyria .”
verses.meaning: Names the developmental threshold of inner awareness — the term identifies the moment before a child knows how to cry out for parents as the timeframe for the predicted conquest.

verses.verse_text: Deu 19:4 “ This is the provision for the manslayer , who by fleeing there may save his life . If anyone kills his neighbor unintentionally without having hated him in the past —
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner state of intent — absence of da.at (without having hated) is the moral condition that determines the act's character. Same pattern as Deu 4:42.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:42 that the manslayer might flee there , anyone who kills his neighbor unintentionally , without being at enmity with him in time past ; he may flee to one of these cities and save his life :
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner state of intent — killing without da.at is killing without the moral knowledge/intent of enmity. The presence or absence of da.at determines culpability.

verses.verse_text: Jos 20:3 that the manslayer who strikes any person without intent or unknowingly may flee there . They shall be for you a refuge from the avenger of blood .
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner state of intent — killing without da.at is unintentional. Same pattern as Deu 4:42.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:39 And as for your little ones , who you said would become a prey , and your children , who today have no knowledge of good or evil , they shall go in there . And to them I will give it , and they shall possess it .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge that young children lack — an inner developmental state, not culpable ignorance.

verse_context_groups.label: Settled inner conviction (knowing as certainty held within)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names knowing as a settled inner conviction — the 'we know that' pattern of the Pauline letters; Job's sustained convictions about God, his situation, and his Redeemer; Qoheleth's recognitions about what he has perceived. The phenomenon is not the relational knowing of God (VCG04) but the inner state of holding something as certain. Inner-being phenomenon: inner certainty or conviction.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Rom 8:28 And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good , for those who are called according to his purpose .
anchor_verse.meaning: oida names the settled convictional knowing that sustains through suffering — love of God grounds the knowing.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good.' The most recognisable instance of the oida-conviction pattern: a settled inner knowing held by the community, grounding their orientation amid suffering. The 'we know that' form is the paradigm of the group — knowledge as an inner certainty that shapes how life is navigated.

verses.verse_text: Rom 2:2 We know that the judgment of God rightly falls on those who practice such things .
verses.meaning: oida names settled inner conviction about divine judgment — a conviction that orients moral life.

verses.verse_text: Rom 3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says it speaks to those who are under the law , so that every mouth may be stopped , and the whole world may be held accountable to God .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner knowing of the law's function — conviction that shapes understanding of the whole human condition.

verses.verse_text: Rom 5:3 Not only that , but we rejoice in our sufferings , knowing that suffering produces endurance ,
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction that sustains rejoicing in suffering — inner knowledge of the process.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:9 We know that Christ , being raised from the dead , will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction of resurrection — the knowledge that Christ's death is final and unrepeated.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:16 Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves , you are slaves of the one whom you obey , either of sin , which leads to death , or of obedience , which leads to righteousness ?
verses.meaning: oida names the inner knowledge of the law of moral slavery — conviction about how allegiance works.

verses.verse_text: Rom 7:14 For we know that the law is spiritual , but I am of the flesh , sold under sin .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about the law's nature — inner knowing that frames the conflict Paul will describe.

verses.verse_text: Rom 7:18 For I know that nothing good dwells in me , that is , in my flesh . For I have the desire to do what is right , but not the ability to carry it out.
verses.meaning: oida names the self-knowledge of inner spiritual conflict — awareness of the flesh as the seat of inability.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:22 For we know that the whole creation has been groaning together in the pains of childbirth until now .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction about creation's condition — knowledge shared by all who have the Spirit.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:26 Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness . For we do not know what to pray for as we ought , but the Spirit himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words .
verses.meaning: oida names the acknowledged limit of self-knowledge in prayer — the Spirit intercedes because the person's own knowing is insufficient.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:28 And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good , for those who are called according to his purpose .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled convictional knowing that sustains through suffering — love of God grounds the knowing.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:2 God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew . Do you not know what the Scripture says of Elijah , how he appeals to God against Israel ?
verses.meaning: oida names the knowledge from Scripture that should be known — a settled conviction about what the text says.

verses.verse_text: Rom 13:11 Besides this you know the time , that the hour has come for you to wake from sleep . For salvation is nearer to us now than when we first believed .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction about the eschatological moment — knowledge that shapes conduct.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:14 I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself , but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean .
verses.meaning: oida names the inner conviction about ritual purity — a settled knowing in the Lord that grounds the position.

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:29 I know that when I come to you I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ .
verses.meaning: oida names Paul's settled inner confidence about his coming visit — knowledge held as conviction.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:16 (I did baptize also the household of Stephanas . Beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else .)
verses.meaning: Names the limit of Paul's certain recollection — the term identifies the inner state of not knowing as the honest boundary of his memory about whom he baptised.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:2 For I decided to know nothing among you except Jesus Christ and him crucified .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate inner resolve — the term identifies Paul's decision to know nothing among them except Christ crucified as a chosen orientation of mind.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:11 For who knows a person’s thoughts except the spirit of that person , which is in him ? So also no one comprehends the thoughts of God except the Spirit of God .
verses.meaning: oida names inner self-knowledge — only the person's own spirit knows their thoughts. God's Spirit alone knows God's thoughts. A key statement about the limits and depth of self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:16 Do you not know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in you ?
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge that the community possesses — knowing who they are in Christ transforms their self-understanding.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 5:6 Your boasting is not good . Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump ?
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about moral contagion — an inner knowing that should shape communal discipline.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:2 Or do you not know that the saints will judge the world ? And if the world is to be judged by you , are you incompetent to try trivial cases ?
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of the saints' eschatological role — knowing this should change how they handle disputes.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:3 Do you not know that we are to judge angels ? How much more , then, matters pertaining to this life !
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of the community's eschatological standing — knowing this transforms self-assessment.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:9 Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God ? Do not be deceived : neither the sexually immoral , nor idolaters , nor adulterers , nor men who practice homosexuality ,
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of the kingdom's character — knowing who does and does not inherit shapes moral orientation.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:15 Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute ? Never !
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of bodily union with Christ — knowing this makes certain acts impossible.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:16 Or do you not know that he who is joined to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For , as it is written , “The two will become one flesh .”
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of bodily union — knowing how union works shapes how it must be guarded.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:1 Now concerning food offered to idols : we know that “ all of us possess knowledge .” This “ knowledge ” puffs up , but love builds up .
verses.meaning: oida names knowledge divorced from love — a claim to knowing that inflates rather than builds. The tension between knowledge and love is explicit.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:4 Therefore , as to the eating of food offered to idols , we know that “an idol has no real existence ,” and that “there is no God but one .”
verses.meaning: oida names the settled theological conviction — inner knowledge about the nature of idols.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 9:13 Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple , and those who serve at the altar share in the sacrificial offerings ?
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner knowing of a practice — used to ground an argument about apostolic rights.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 9:24 Do you not know that in a race all the runners run , but only one receives the prize ? So run that you may obtain it.
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner knowing of how competition works — used to motivate purposeful spiritual effort.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 11:3 But I want you to understand that the head of every man is Christ , the head of a wife is her husband , and the head of Christ is God .
verses.meaning: oida names the inner conviction about order — knowledge that should shape community practice.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:2 You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols , however you were led .
verses.meaning: oida names the inner knowledge of their own past — conviction about what they were before Christ.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:2 And if I have prophetic powers , and understand all mysteries and all knowledge , and if I have all faith , so as to remove mountains , but have not love , I am nothing .
verses.meaning: oida names knowledge divorced from love — understanding all mysteries without love is inner emptiness. The most explicit statement of knowledge without love.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:11 but if I do not know the meaning of the language , I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me .
verses.meaning: Names the prerequisite for meaningful communication — the term identifies knowing the meaning of a language as the inner comprehension without which speech becomes mere noise.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:16 Otherwise , if you give thanks with your spirit , how can anyone in the position of an outsider say “ Amen ” to your thanksgiving when he does not know what you are saying ?
verses.meaning: Names the inner state of the uninitiated hearer — the term identifies the outsider's lack of comprehension of what is being said as the condition that makes uninterpreted tongues unhelpful.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:58 Therefore , my beloved brothers , be steadfast , immovable , always abounding in the work of the Lord , knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction that sustains labour — inner knowing of the resurrection's guarantee.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 16:15 Now I urge you , brothers —you know that the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia , and that they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints —
verses.meaning: Names what the community already knows — the term identifies their inner awareness of Stephanas's household and its service as established knowledge Paul appeals to.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:7 Our hope for you is unshaken , for we know that as you share in our sufferings , you will also share in our comfort .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about the logic of suffering and comfort — inner knowing that grounds hope.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:14 knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus and bring us with you into his presence .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction of resurrection — inner knowledge that sustains ministry through suffering.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:1 For we know that if the tent that is our earthly home is destroyed , we have a building from God , a house not made with hands , eternal in the heavens .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about the eternal body — inner knowing that relativises present suffering.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:6 So we are always of good courage . We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord ,
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction about present existence — knowledge that shapes the orientation of hope.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:16 From now on , therefore , we regard no one according to the flesh . Even though we once regarded Christ according to the flesh , we regard him thus no longer .
verses.meaning: oida names the new inner knowing — regard shaped by resurrection rather than by worldly categories.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 9:2 for I know your readiness , of which I boast about you to the people of Macedonia , saying that Achaia has been ready since last year . And your zeal has stirred up most of them.
verses.meaning: oida names Paul's knowledge of the Corinthian community — inner knowing of their generosity that he makes public.

verses.verse_text: Gal 2:16 yet we know that a person is not justified by works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ , so we also have believed in Christ Jesus , in order to be justified by faith in Christ and not by works of the law , because by works of the law no one will be justified .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled theological conviction — inner knowing that grounds the argument for faith.

verses.verse_text: Gal 4:8 Formerly , when you did not know God , you were enslaved to those that by nature are not gods .
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of God — not knowing God defines the pre-conversion condition of enslavement. Knowing God defines freedom.

verses.verse_text: Gal 4:13 You know it was because of a bodily ailment that I preached the gospel to you at first ,
verses.meaning: Names what the Galatians know from experience — the term identifies their inner awareness of the bodily ailment that occasioned Paul's first preaching to them.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:18 having the eyes of your hearts enlightened , that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints ,
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of calling and inheritance — the eyes of the heart enlightened to know.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:5 For you may be sure of this , that everyone who is sexually immoral or impure , or who is covetous ( that is , an idolater ), has no inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God .
verses.meaning: oida names settled inner certainty about moral consequence — conviction that shapes conduct.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:8 knowing that whatever good anyone does , this he will receive back from the Lord , whether he is a bondservant or is free .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about divine recompense — inner knowing that motivates faithful service.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:9 Masters , do the same to them , and stop your threatening , knowing that he who is both their Master and yours is in heaven , and that there is no partiality with him .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about shared accountability — inner knowing that shapes how power is used.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:21 So that you also may know how I am and what I am doing , Tychicus the beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord will tell you everything .
verses.meaning: Names the purpose of Tychicus's mission — the term identifies making the recipients know Paul's situation as the inner goal of sending a trusted messenger.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:1 For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those at Laodicea and for all who have not seen me face to face ,
verses.meaning: oida names Paul's desire for the community to know his inner condition — transparency about the apostolic inner life.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:24 knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward . You are serving the Lord Christ .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about eschatological reward — inner knowing that makes service worth giving.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:1 Masters , treat your bondservants justly and fairly , knowing that you also have a Master in heaven .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about common accountability — inner knowing that shapes how authority is exercised.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:25 For I know that my Redeemer lives , and at the last he will stand upon the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction of covenantal hope — a settled inner knowing of the Redeemer's existence that sustains through suffering.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:18 Behold, I have prepared my case ; I know that I shall be in the right .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction of his own righteousness — self-knowledge of moral standing.

verses.verse_text: Job 10:13 Yet these things you hid in your heart ; I know that this was your purpose.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction about God's hidden purposes — a dark knowing of divine intentionality.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:7 I said , ‘Let days speak , and many years teach wisdom .’
verses.meaning: Names the inner acquisition of wisdom — the term identifies the teaching that many years impart as a form of knowing that Elihu had deferred to.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:22 For I do not know how to flatter , else my Maker would soon take me away .
verses.meaning: ya.da names self-knowledge of one's own speech — Elihu's inner honesty about his incapacity for flattery.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:2 “Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction — self-knowledge of a moral-theological truth about God's justice.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:28 I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction about how God will treat him — self-knowledge of his vulnerability.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:27 “ Behold , I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner knowledge of his friends' hidden motives — seeing through their arguments to their purpose.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:34 At the end of the days I , Nebuchadnezzar , lifted my eyes to heaven , and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High , and praised and honored him who lives forever , for his dominion is an everlasting dominion , and his kingdom endures from generation to generation ;
verses.meaning: man.da names reason as the inner defining capacity of humanness — its loss is the loss of personhood; its return restores dignity. A powerful use: reason as the inner seat of the human person.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:36 At the same time my reason returned to me, and for the glory of my kingdom , my majesty and splendor returned to me. My counselors and my lords sought me , and I was established in my kingdom , and still more greatness was added to me .
verses.meaning: man.da names the same restored capacity — the same verse pattern. Reason's return is immediately linked to restored honour and greatness.

verses.verse_text: Gen 19:8 Behold , I have two daughters who have not known any man . Let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please . Only do nothing to these men , for they have come under the shelter of my roof .”
verses.meaning: Names the virgin state — the term identifies Lot's daughters as women who have not known a man, offered in place of the visitors.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:12 Though a sinner does evil a hundred times and prolongs his life, yet I know that it will be well with those who fear God , because they fear before him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Qoheleth's inner conviction of God's justice — a settled inner knowing that transcends the apparent contradictions.

verses.verse_text: Est 4:14 For if you keep silent at this time , relief and deliverance will rise for the Jews from another place , but you and your father’s house will perish . And who knows whether you have not come to the kingdom for such a time as this ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the open recognition of divine providential knowing — who can know God's purposes? The question assumes God acts with knowledge even when hidden.

verses.verse_text: Isa 50:7 But the Lord God helps me; therefore I have not been disgraced ; therefore I have set my face like a flint , and I know that I shall not be put to shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the Servant's inner conviction of vindication — settled inner knowledge of God's support.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:2 What you know , I also know; I am not inferior to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same knowing — Job's self-assertion that his inner knowledge is equal to theirs.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:23 He wanders abroad for bread , saying, ‘ Where is it?’ He knows that a day of darkness is ready at his hand ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names the wicked man's inner knowledge of his own peril — a dark self-awareness.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:9 What do you know that we do not know ? What do you understand that is not clear to us ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being disputed — a challenge to Job's claim to inner knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:6 know then that God has put me in the wrong and closed his net about me.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner covenantal conviction Job urges — a recognition of his situation before God.

verses.verse_text: Job 30:23 For I know that you will bring me to death and to the house appointed for all living .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner certainty about his approaching death — self-knowledge of his end.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:33 Will he then make repayment to suit you, because you reject it? For you must choose , and not I; therefore declare what you know .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge Job is challenged to express — inner moral knowing called to account.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:27 Behold , this we have searched out ; it is true . Hear , and know it for your good.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being urged on Job — receiving the wisdom of experience as covenantal knowing.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:21 I am blameless ; I regard not myself ; I loathe my life .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's self-assessment — he does not regard (know) himself in a way that troubles him about his integrity. A complex use.

verses.verse_text: Judg 17:13 Then Micah said , “ Now I know that the Lord will prosper me, because I have a Levite as priest .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Micah's inner conviction about God's favour — a covenantal knowing based on having a Levite priest (though misguided).

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:14 The wise person has his eyes in his head , but the fool walks in darkness . And yet I perceived that the same event happens to all of them.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner recognition of a pattern — Qoheleth's self-knowing of a bitter truth about mortality.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:12 I perceived that there is nothing better for them than to be joyful and to do good as long as they live ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner recognition of a practical wisdom — what Qoheleth has come to know about human flourishing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:14 I perceived that whatever God does endures forever ; nothing can be added to it, nor anything taken from it. God has done it, so that people fear before him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's permanence — Qoheleth's inner knowing of divine sovereignty.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:21 Who knows whether the spirit of man goes upward and the spirit of the beast goes down into the earth ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the limit of self-knowledge before death — no one can know what happens to the spirit.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of one's own limitation — the king could no longer know how to receive counsel.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:22 Your heart knows that many times you yourself have cursed others .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the heart's inner self-knowledge of its own failures — the honest inner recognition that one has done what one is condemning.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:17 then I saw all the work of God , that man cannot find out the work that is done under the sun . However much man may toil in seeking , he will not find it out. Even though a wise man claims to know , he cannot find it out.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the claimed knowledge that cannot reach God's work — the limit of human knowing before divine mystery.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:1 But all this I laid to heart , examining it all , how the righteous and the wise and their deeds are in the hand of God . Whether it is love or hate , man does not know ; both are before him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the impenetrable limit of self-knowledge before God's disposition — the inner uncertainty before divine hiddenness.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:12 For man does not know his time . Like fish that are taken in an evil net , and like birds that are caught in a snare , so the children of man are snared at an evil time , when it suddenly falls upon them.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner ignorance of one's own end — a universal human condition.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:5 For the living know that they will die , but the dead know nothing , and they have no more reward , for the memory of them is forgotten .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner knowledge of mortality in the living — knowing one's end is a distinguishing mark of being alive.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:12 He said to them , “ Pick me up and hurl me into the sea ; then the sea will quiet down for you, for I know it is because of me that this great tempest has come upon you .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Jonah's self-knowledge of his own culpability — settled inner recognition of his responsibility.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:10 The heart knows its own bitterness , and no stranger shares its joy .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the heart's self-knowledge of its own inner states — bitterness and joy are known inwardly and cannot be fully shared.

verse_context_groups.label: Culpable ignorance (agnoeō and equivalents)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names ignorance that is culpable, structural, or judicially significant — the agnoeō (G0050) pattern: those who do not know because they have not perceived, those whose ignorance becomes the mechanism of their actions, those Paul corrects by disclosure. Includes innocent ignorance where the absence rather than the culpability is what the term names. Inner-being phenomenon: inner state of ignorance with moral or judicial significance.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Rom 10:3 For , being ignorant of the righteousness of God , and seeking to establish their own , they did not submit to God’s righteousness .
anchor_verse.meaning: agnoeō names the inner ignorance of God's righteousness as the root of Israel's failure — not knowing God's righteousness, they pursue self-righteousness instead.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'For, being ignorant of the righteousness of God, and seeking to establish their own, they did not submit to God's righteousness.' The most theologically weighted instance of agnoeō: ignorance of God's righteousness is the inner root of the whole pattern of self-righteous religious effort. Names the content of the ignorance (God's righteousness), the alternative inner pursuit it produces (self-righteousness), and the consequence (non-submission).

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:32 But they did not understand the saying , and were afraid to ask him .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the disciples' inner failure of comprehension — they do not understand the passion prediction. P-status: the span is not carrying inner-being content distinctively beyond the suniēmi parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:45 But they did not understand this saying , and it was concealed from them , so that they might not perceive it . And they were afraid to ask him about this saying .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the judicial concealment of understanding — the disciples could not grasp what was being said. P-status.

verses.verse_text: Act 13:27 For those who live in Jerusalem and their rulers , because they did not recognize him nor understand the utterances of the prophets , which are read every Sabbath , fulfilled them by condemning him.
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the culpable inner failure to recognise Jesus — the Jerusalem rulers' ignorance of both Jesus and the prophets. This ignorance becomes the mechanism of fulfilment.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:23 For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship , I found also an altar with this inscription , ‘To the unknown god .’ What therefore you worship as unknown , this I proclaim to you .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the worshippers' inner ignorance of the one they worship — the Athenians have a devotion without knowing its object. Paul names this as the condition he addresses.

verses.verse_text: Rom 2:4 Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience , not knowing that God’s kindness is meant to lead you to repentance ?
verses.meaning: agnoeō names culpable ignorance of God's purpose — the presumptuous person does not know that divine patience is intended as a call to repentance.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:3 Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death ?
verses.meaning: Poses a rhetorical challenge to an assumed ignorance about baptism's union with Christ's death — the term identifies the inner failure of understanding that would make the doctrinal point necessary.

verses.verse_text: Rom 7:1 Or do you not know , brothers — for I am speaking to those who know the law — that the law is binding on a person only as long as he lives ?
verses.meaning: Challenges an assumed ignorance about the law's binding scope — the term frames the argument as correcting a deficit of understanding in the audience.

verses.verse_text: Rom 10:3 For , being ignorant of the righteousness of God , and seeking to establish their own , they did not submit to God’s righteousness .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the inner ignorance of God's righteousness as the root of Israel's failure — not knowing God's righteousness, they pursue self-righteousness instead.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:25 Lest you be wise in your own sight, I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery , brothers : a partial hardening has come upon Israel , until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in .
verses.meaning: Names the self-inflating inner disposition Paul warns against — the term identifies a conceit that makes the audience susceptible to missing the mystery now being disclosed.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 10:1 For I do not want you to be unaware , brothers , that our fathers were all under the cloud , and all passed through the sea ,
verses.meaning: Names the state of unawareness Paul is preventing — the term introduces a corrective disclosure about Israel's wilderness experience.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts , brothers , I do not want you to be uninformed .
verses.meaning: Names the uninformed inner state Paul addresses — the term opens a corrective teaching about spiritual gifts.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:38 If anyone does not recognize this, he is not recognized .
verses.meaning: Names the condition of non-recognition — the term marks out a person whose failure to acknowledge the prophetic word results in their own being unacknowledged.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:8 For we do not want you to be unaware , brothers , of the affliction we experienced in Asia . For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself .
verses.meaning: Names the state of unawareness Paul wishes to prevent — the term frames the account of severe affliction as a necessary disclosure to the community.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names culpable ignorance of Satan's strategies — Paul and his companions are not in this condition; they know Satan's designs. The inner knowing protects against being outwitted.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:9 as unknown , and yet well known ; as dying , and behold , we live ; as punished , and yet not killed ;
verses.meaning: Characterises the apostolic condition of being unrecognised — the term names the outward obscurity that conceals the inner reality of ongoing life.

verses.verse_text: Gal 1:22 And I was still unknown in person to the churches of Judea that are in Christ .
verses.meaning: Names Paul's state of personal anonymity to the Judean churches — the term describes an absence of inner recognition or familiarity on the part of those communities.

verses.verse_text: 1Th 4:13 But we do not want you to be uninformed , brothers , about those who are asleep , that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope .
verses.meaning: Names the uninformed inner state Paul is correcting — the term introduces eschatological instruction about the dead that is meant to reshape inner orientation away from grief.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 1:13 though formerly I was a blasphemer , persecutor , and insolent opponent . But I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief ,
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the culpable inner ignorance of Paul's pre-conversion persecution — not knowing what he was doing becomes the grounds of mercy.

verses.verse_text: Heb 5:2 He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward , since he himself is beset with weakness .
verses.meaning: Names the condition of those who are ignorant and morally wayward — the term characterises the high priest's capacity for gentle dealing as appropriate to the inner frailty of this group.

verses.verse_text: 2Pe 2:12 But these , like irrational animals , creatures of instinct, born to be caught and destroyed , blaspheming about matters of which they are ignorant , will also be destroyed in their destruction ,
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the culpable inner ignorance of false teachers — they blaspheme what they do not know, and their ignorance does not excuse them.

verses.verse_text: Jon 4:11 And should not I pity Nineveh , that great city , in which there are more than 120,000 persons who do not know their right hand from their left , and also much cattle ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral immaturity — the Ninevites who cannot distinguish right from left lack the inner moral knowing that would make them accountable.

verse_context_groups.label: Practical and experiential knowing
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the ordinary, practical knowing of persons, facts, situations, and skills — knowing where someone went, knowing that a person is trustworthy, knowing a skill, recognising someone. This is knowing as everyday awareness and competence, not covenantal or moral. The largest group by verse count, spanning narrative throughout Genesis–Malachi and the NT. Inner-being phenomenon: practical inner awareness and recognition.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Gen 8:11 And the dove came back to him in the evening , and behold , in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf . So Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth .
anchor_verse.meaning: Names the inference drawn from evidence — the term identifies Noah's inner recognition that the waters had subsided, based on the dove's return with an olive leaf.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'And the dove came back to him in the evening, and behold, in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf. So Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth.' The phenomenon is unmistakably clear: inference from evidence produces inner knowledge of a fact about the world. The term names the cognitive movement from sign to certainty — simple, practical, grounded. A clean instantiation of the phenomenon without covenantal or moral overlay.

verses.verse_text: Gen 12:11 When he was about to enter Egypt , he said to Sarai his wife , “ I know that you are a woman beautiful in appearance ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names Abram's self-aware assessment of his situation — knowledge of a fact that produces anxiety and strategic deception.

verses.verse_text: Gen 25:27 When the boys grew up, Esau was a skillful hunter , a man of the field , while Jacob was a quiet man , dwelling in tents .
verses.meaning: Names a form of competence — the term identifies Esau as a man who knew or was skilled in hunting, characterising his practical expertise.

verses.verse_text: Gen 27:2 He said , “ Behold , I am old ; I do not know the day of my death .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Isaac's self-awareness of the limit of his knowledge — he knows he is old but not when he will die.

verses.verse_text: Gen 28:16 Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said , “ Surely the Lord is in this place , and I did not know it.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence and then presence of covenantal awareness — Jacob had not known God's presence in the place until the dream.

verses.verse_text: Gen 29:5 He said to them, “Do you know Laban the son of Nahor ?” They said , “We know him.”
verses.meaning: Names the inner recognition of a person — the term identifies the herdsmen's familiarity with Laban as the basis for Jacob's inquiry.

verses.verse_text: Gen 30:29 Jacob said to him, “ You yourself know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me .
verses.meaning: Names the self-evident inner awareness — the term identifies what Laban himself knows about how Jacob served him and how the flock fared.

verses.verse_text: Gen 31:6 You know that I have served your father with all my strength ,
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged knowledge — the term identifies what Rachel and Leah already know about Jacob's whole-hearted service to their father.

verses.verse_text: Gen 31:32 Anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live . In the presence of our kinsmen point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Jacob's ignorance of Rachel's theft of the household gods.

verses.verse_text: Gen 33:13 But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail , and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me. If they are driven hard for one day , all the flocks will die .
verses.meaning: Names the knowledge Jacob appeals to — the term identifies his lord's inner awareness of the frailty of the children and nursing animals as the reason for slow travel.

verses.verse_text: Gen 38:9 But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he went in to his brother’s wife he would waste the semen on the ground , so as not to give offspring to his brother .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Onan's knowledge of the levirate situation — moral knowledge that guides (or in this case, subverts) right action.

verses.verse_text: Gen 39:6 So he left all that he had in Joseph’s charge , and because of him he had no concern about anything but the food he ate . Now Joseph was handsome in form and appearance .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate withholding of oversight — the term identifies Potiphar's choice not to know or concern himself with anything in the house beyond his food.

verses.verse_text: Gen 39:8 But he refused and said to his master’s wife , “ Behold , because of me my master has no concern about anything in the house , and he has put everything that he has in my charge .
verses.meaning: Names Potiphar's absence of oversight — the term identifies the master's inner withdrawal from concern about the household as the context for Joseph's authority.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:21 but when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them , for they were still as ugly as at the beginning . Then I awoke .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of visible trace — the term identifies the impossibility of knowing that the gaunt cows had consumed the fat ones because they appeared unchanged.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:31 and the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of the famine that will follow , for it will be very severe .
verses.meaning: Names the future invisibility of prosperity — the term identifies how the coming plenty will not be known or recognised in the land because the famine will be so severe.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:23 They did not know that Joseph understood them, for there was an interpreter between them .
verses.meaning: Names the brothers' absence of awareness — the term identifies their failure to know that Joseph understood their speech, since an interpreter stood between them.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:33 Then the man , the lord of the land , said to us, ‘ By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers with me, and take grain for the famine of your households , and go your way.
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of discernment — the term identifies Joseph's stated basis for knowing whether the brothers are honest: leaving one behind.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:34 Bring your youngest brother to me. Then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land .’”
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of verification — the term identifies knowing the brothers are not spies as the condition for releasing the imprisoned one.

verses.verse_text: Gen 43:7 They replied , “The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred , saying , ‘Is your father still alive ? Do you have another brother ?’ What we told him was in answer to these questions . Could we in any way know that he would say , ‘Bring your brother down ’?”
verses.meaning: Names the impossibility of foreknowledge — the term identifies the brothers' inability to have known in advance that Joseph would demand their youngest brother.

verses.verse_text: Gen 43:22 and we have brought other money down with us to buy food . We do not know who put our money in our sacks .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies the brothers' genuine ignorance about who placed the money in their sacks.

verses.verse_text: Gen 44:15 Joseph said to them, “ What deed is this that you have done ? Do you not know that a man like me can indeed practice divination ?”
verses.meaning: Names the capacity for insight — the term identifies Joseph's ability to practise divination as a known inner capability he appeals to rhetorically.

verses.verse_text: Gen 44:27 Then your servant my father said to us, ‘ You know that my wife bore me two sons.
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged knowledge — the term identifies what the father himself knows: that his wife bore him two sons.

verses.verse_text: Gen 47:6 The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land . Let them settle in the land of Goshen , and if you know any able men among them, put them in charge of my livestock .”
verses.meaning: Names the practical inner knowledge — the term identifies the instruction to identify able men among the brothers based on known competence.

verses.verse_text: Exo 1:8 Now there arose a new king over Egypt , who did not know Joseph .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of historical awareness — the term identifies the new king's failure to know Joseph, which underlies his oppression of Israel.

verses.verse_text: Exo 2:14 He answered , “ Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian ?” Then Moses was afraid , and thought , “ Surely the thing is known .”
verses.meaning: Names the unwilling inner realisation — the term identifies Moses' recognition that the killing had become known, prompting his fear.

verses.verse_text: Num 10:31 And he said , “ Please do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness , and you will serve as eyes for us.
verses.meaning: Names the practical navigational knowledge — the term identifies Hobab's inner familiarity with the wilderness as the resource Israel needs for camping.

verses.verse_text: Num 11:16 Then the Lord said to Moses , “ Gather for me seventy men of the elders of Israel , whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the tent of meeting , and let them take their stand there with you .
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of selection — the term identifies Moses' prior knowledge of the elders as the basis for God's instruction to gather them.

verses.verse_text: Num 20:14 Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom : “ Thus says your brother Israel : You know all the hardship that we have met :
verses.meaning: Names the shared historical knowledge — the term identifies Edom's inner awareness of all the hardship Israel has experienced as the basis for the appeal.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:34 Then Balaam said to the angel of the Lord , “I have sinned , for I did not know that you stood in the road against me. Now therefore, if it is evil in your sight , I will turn back .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Balaam's genuine ignorance that the angel stood in his way as the ground of his admission of sin.

verses.verse_text: Deu 3:19 Only your wives , your little ones , and your livestock (I know that you have much livestock ) shall remain in the cities that I have given you ,
verses.meaning: Names God's inner awareness — the term identifies God's knowledge of the size of Israel's livestock as the basis for the exemption granted.

verses.verse_text: Deu 7:15 And the Lord will take away from you all sickness , and none of the evil diseases of Egypt , which you knew , will he inflict on you, but he will lay them on all who hate you .
verses.meaning: Names the shared inner experience — the term identifies the evil diseases of Egypt as things Israel has known through direct experience.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:2 a people great and tall , the sons of the Anakim , whom you know , and of whom you have heard it said, ‘ Who can stand before the sons of Anak ?’
verses.meaning: Names prior knowledge through testimony — the term identifies the people's existing awareness of the Anakim through report as the basis of Moses' challenge.

verses.verse_text: Deu 20:20 Only the trees that you know are not trees for food you may destroy and cut down , that you may build siegeworks against the city that makes war with you, until it falls .
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of identification — the term identifies knowing which trees are non-food trees as the inner basis for lawful destruction in siege.

verses.verse_text: Deu 21:1 “ If in the land that the Lord your God is giving you to possess someone is found slain , lying in the open country , and it is not known who killed him ,
verses.meaning: Names the absence of knowledge as a legal condition — the term identifies the unknown identity of the killer as the situation that triggers the heifer ritual.

verses.verse_text: Deu 22:2 And if he does not live near you and you do not know who he is, you shall bring it home to your house , and it shall stay with you until your brother seeks it. Then you shall restore it to him .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of familiarity — the term identifies not knowing who the owner is as the condition that requires temporary custody of the lost animal.

verses.verse_text: Deu 28:33 A nation that you have not known shall eat up the fruit of your ground and of all your labors , and you shall be only oppressed and crushed continually ,
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the consuming nation as one Israel has not known, intensifying the alienation of the curse.

verses.verse_text: Deu 28:36 “The Lord will bring you and your king whom you set over you to a nation that neither you nor your fathers have known . And there you shall serve other gods of wood and stone .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the nation of exile as one neither Israel nor their fathers have known, deepening the estrangement.

verses.verse_text: Deu 28:64 “And the Lord will scatter you among all peoples , from one end of the earth to the other , and there you shall serve other gods of wood and stone , which neither you nor your fathers have known .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the gods of exile as those neither Israel nor their fathers have known.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:16 “ You know how we lived in the land of Egypt , and how we came through the midst of the nations through which you passed .
verses.meaning: Names the shared experiential knowledge — the term identifies Israel's firsthand inner knowledge of life in Egypt as the basis for the covenant appeal.

verses.verse_text: Deu 33:9 who said of his father and mother , ‘I regard them not ’; he disowned his brothers and ignored his children . For they observed your word and kept your covenant .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate setting aside of inner recognition — the term identifies Levi's act of not regarding or knowing his own family as the mark of covenant loyalty.

verses.verse_text: Deu 34:6 and he buried him in the valley in the land of Moab opposite Beth-peor ; but no one knows the place of his burial to this day .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of locatable knowledge — the term identifies the fact that no one knows the place of Moses' burial to the present day.

verses.verse_text: Jos 2:4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. And she said , “ True , the men came to me, but I did not know where they were from.
verses.meaning: Names the claimed absence of knowledge — the term identifies Rahab's stated ignorance of where the men came from as her cover for concealing them.

verses.verse_text: Jos 2:5 And when the gate was about to be closed at dark , the men went out. I do not know where the men went . Pursue them quickly , for you will overtake them .”
verses.meaning: Names the claimed absence of directional knowledge — the term identifies Rahab's stated ignorance of where the men went as part of her misdirection.

verses.verse_text: Jos 8:14 And as soon as the king of Ai saw this, he and all his people , the men of the city , hurried and went out early to the appointed place toward the Arabah to meet Israel in battle . But he did not know that there was an ambush against him behind the city .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of tactical awareness — the term identifies the king of Ai's failure to know that an ambush had been set behind the city.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:1 Now these are the nations that the Lord left , to test Israel by them, that is, all in Israel who had not experienced all the wars in Canaan .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of experiential knowledge — the term identifies the lack of experience of warfare in Canaan as the condition of the generation left to be tested.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:2 It was only in order that the generations of the people of Israel might know war, to teach war to those who had not known it before .
verses.meaning: Names the inner acquisition of practical knowledge — the term identifies teaching war to those who had not known it as the purpose of leaving the nations.

verses.verse_text: Judg 8:16 And he took the elders of the city , and he took thorns of the wilderness and briers and with them taught the men of Succoth a lesson.
verses.meaning: Names the act of teaching through painful discipline — the term identifies Gideon's instruction of the men of Succoth by means of thorns and briers.

verses.verse_text: Judg 15:11 Then 3,000 men of Judah went down to the cleft of the rock of Etam , and said to Samson , “Do you not know that the Philistines are rulers over us? What then is this that you have done to us?” And he said to them, “ As they did to me, so have I done to them .”
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged knowledge — the term identifies the Philistines' rule as a fact the men of Judah assume Samson already knows.

verses.verse_text: Judg 16:9 Now she had men lying in ambush in an inner chamber . And she said to him, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson !” But he snapped the bowstrings , as a thread of flax snaps when it touches the fire . So the secret of his strength was not known .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of disclosure — the term identifies the secret of Samson's strength as something not made known, despite the trap set.

verses.verse_text: Judg 18:14 Then the five men who had gone to scout out the country of Laish said to their brothers , “Do you know that in these houses there are an ephod , household gods , a carved image , and a metal image ? Now therefore consider what you will do .”
verses.meaning: Names the inner awareness of what exists — the term identifies the five scouts' knowledge of the cultic objects in the house as the basis for their counsel.

verses.verse_text: Judg 20:34 And there came against Gibeah 10,000 chosen men out of all Israel , and the battle was hard , but the Benjaminites did not know that disaster was close upon them.
verses.meaning: Names the absence of tactical awareness — the term identifies the Benjaminites' failure to know that disaster was close upon them.

verses.verse_text: Neh 2:16 And the officials did not know where I had gone or what I was doing , and I had not yet told the Jews , the priests , the nobles , the officials , and the rest who were to do the work .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies the officials' failure to know where Nehemiah had gone or what he was doing.
